They include immediate access to a structured onboarding resource center, along with self-paced learning enablement to help teams build foundational knowledge at their own pace. Transparency on entitlements and scope is also enhanced through the supplement guides for SAP Cloud ERP Private.

During this early “Onboarding” phase, embedded launch activities bring structure and direction to the experience by:

  • Introducing the delivery model for the RISE with SAP journey through personalized calls, webinars, and hands-on sessions
  • Aligning teams on roles, phases, and expectations
  • Establishing early project setup and governance foundations
  • Connecting methodology, tools, and responsibilities into a coherent way of working
  • Enabling teams through guided, role-specific learning paths

At this stage, speed isn’t the most significant risk. Misalignment is.

By creating a shared understanding of how the transformation is executed, embedded launch activities reduce early misunderstandings that can later turn into costly course corrections. Teams gain clarity not only on the destination, but also on how they will move forward together.

In practice, this early alignment ensures momentum is built on the right foundation, before small issues grow into larger challenges.


Figure 1: Onboarding support enabled by embedded launch activities


Transformation preparation service: Moving from strategy to action

Embedded launch activities establish a strong foundation, but transformation rarely becomes simpler once onboarding is complete. This is often the moment when the most consequential decisions begin to take shape.

The transformation preparation service for SAP Business Suite turns strategy into an executable plan. It bridges the space between onboarding and realization, helping ensure the project is ready to move forward with the right scope, setup, and governance.

During this phase, the service provides structured guidance through:

  • Kickoff sessions to define transformation scope and road map
  • Tailored scoping workshops covering topics, such as industry needs, validation, and AI capabilities
  • Deep-dive workshops with SAP experts on technical and strategic topics, including the clean core approach
  • Introduction and provisioning of subscribed SAP solution entitlements
  • Demonstrations of AI use cases and SAP Build solutions
  • Activation of the Joule solution through the setup service
  • Creation and handover of a governance playbook with clear, actionable guidelines

More than a one-time checkpoint, the transformation preparation service for SAP Business Suite is designed to support customers at key decision points when uncertainty increases and the experience makes the difference.

SAP customers often value this phase because it brings an external, seasoned perspective. It creates space to pause, validate direction, and move forward with intention rather than reacting under pressure.


Figure 2: Offerings included in the transformation preparation service for SAP Business Suite (Learn more)

The real value: Building confidence when it counts

At its core, expert guidance brings SAP’s delivery experience directly into the customer journey, from the very beginning and across the full transformation lifecycle.

What expert guidance ultimately delivers is not control. It’s confidence that:

  • The journey starts on solid ground
  • Critical decisions are made with the right perspective and timing
  • Teams are supported, aligned, and never navigating complexity on their own

In large-scale transformations, that confidence is often what separates organizations that simply follow a plan from those that successfully bring it to life.

To learn more about RISE with SAP Methodology, read our in-depth overview.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RISE with SAP Methodology - Blog-series 

Discover the 5-part blog series:

  • Blog 1/5 (December 3rd, 2025)
    RISE with SAP Methodology: A practical Guide to Modernizing with Confidence
  • Blog 2/5 (December 10th, 2025)
    Deep Dive: Inside the framework of RISE with SAP Methodology
  • Blog 3/5 (January 14th, 2026)
    The Integrated Toolchain in a Nutshell
  • Blog 4/5 (January 28th, 2026)
    Expert Guidance: Your Guided Transformation Accelerator
  • Blog 5/5 (February 11th, 2026)
    The Combined Power of SAP Joule and AI for the RISE with SAP Methodology

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2025: Key Innovations in Sales for the Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
🌟 SAP S/4HANA 2025 Key Innovations in Sales for cloud Private Edition

With the October 2025 release of SAP S/4HANA 2025 Cloud Private Edition, SAP continues to strengthen the Sales line of business through new capabilities that improve performance, automation, and process transparency.
After exploring the latest updates, I’ve summarized some of the highlights that deliver the most value—spanning Output Management, extensibility, order processing, returns management, and more.

🔎How to Explore What’s New in SAP S/4HANA Releases

If you want to compare innovations between releases—for example, from S/4HANA 2023 → S/4HANA 2025—the SAP Help Portal and the What's New Viewer remain the most efficient way to explore updates by product, line of business, and topic.

👉You can access the What's New Viewer What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition directly from the SAP Help Portal and filter by:

  • Product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Line of Business: Sales
  • Type: New or Changed
  • Version range: From your current release to a target release

This allows you to quickly identify innovations relevant to your business processes.

Key Sales Innovations in SAP S/4HANA 2025

☘️ Output Management Enhancements (NAST + Output Control)

One of the major improvements in S/4HANA 2025 is SAP’s expanded support for Output Control in combination with legacy NAST‑generated forms.

Preview NAST Forms in Fiori Apps

If you previously used NAST forms and later activated Output Management, you can now preview these legacy forms directly in Fiori, including:

👉  Manage Billing Documents: Document Preview (F0797)

👉  Manage Sales Orders Version 2: Document Preview (F3893) 

No additional configuration is required—your existing NAST setup simply needs to remain active.

Here an example:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Cross-Application Components->Output Control->Manage Application Object Type Activation

In Manage Sales Order – Version 2 ( F3693 ) with a sales order that the form was generated by NAST:

You can preview the form: 

💡Business Value: More flexibility to Customer outputs forms.

 

☘️ New Flexibility for Global Trade Management

In Trading Contracts, you can now choose between:

👉NAST

👉SAP S/4HANA Output Control 

👉Or both, at the contract type level

This gives organizations more flexibility during transitions between output frameworks.

New Output Management for Global Trade Management

By default, the message determination for trading contracts is still NAST so if you decide to use Output Control you should configure first the object type activation for:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Cross-Application Components->Output Control->Manage Application Object Type Activation

And then you can choose by Trading Contract type which Output Control indicator prefer:

SAP Customizing Impl. Guide->Logistics – General->Global Trade Management->Trading Contract->Define Trading Contract Types

💡Business Value: More features for Trading Contracts.

 

☘️ Import Sales Orders: Reference-Based Creation

A highly valuable enhancement: You can now create sales orders with reference to quotations or contracts using the Import Sales Orders app 

The imported spreadsheet now supports:

👉Header reference data

👉Item reference data

👉Full or partial carry-over of preceding document information

The new template include:

💡Business Value: This reduces manual effort, improves data consistency, and accelerates end-to-end sales order creation.

 

☘️ New Extensibility: Sales Order API (A2X, OData V4)

SAP delivers a modernized and more efficient API Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) |built on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). It offers:

👉Faster processing performance

👉Asynchronous execution

👉Ability to return minimal or full responses

👉Full access to pricing details, including subtotals

👉Enhanced filtering for sub-entities (items, partners, values, etc.)

This empowers developers to create more scalable and performant integrations.

To explore available APIs please follow this link: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

💡Business Value: Improves process performance

 

☘️ New CDS Views for Global Trade Management

S/4HANA 2025 introduces additional CDS Views for GTM supporting:

👉Customer and supplier confirmation emails

👉Header-level contract information

👉Output parameter determination

👉Value helps (organizational data, user IDs, contract types, etc.)

💡Business Value: These new views help streamline reporting, automation, and custom extensions. For more information, please see CDS Views for Global Trade Management | SAP Help Portal

 

☘️ Claims, Returns, and Refund Management

I’ve explored into Process Navigator - SAP for Me  into SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and the Customer Returns (BKP) process receives important updates in S/4Hana 2025.

🙌  Determine No Refund for Logistical Follow-Up Activity Receive into Plant

You have the option not to refund the customer when you select the logistical follow-up activity Receive into Plant (0001) in the returns order and later on  you can still create a credit memo request or a delivery free-of-charge with reference to the relevant returns order item.

🙌 Automatic Inspection After Goods Receipt in Customer Returns

You can enter the inspection result when you create a returns order, before the product is returned. If you already know the inspection result before you receive the product and don’t want to go through another inspection after it has been returned, you can ask the system to automatically create and complete the inspection using the inspection data and the next follow-up activity you provide when you create the returns order. As a result, after you receive the product, the system completes the inspection and triggers the specified subsequent logistic steps automatically.

🙌Refurbishment Using Maintenance Order as Follow-Up Activity

A maintenance order is created for returned products to initiate the refurbishment process. The maintenance order is configured as a refurbishment order.

🙌Refurbishment Using Production Order as Follow-Up Activity

A production order is created for returned products to initiate the refurbishment process. The production version specified for the production order must be a specific version that indicates refurbishment.

🙌Quality Notification as a Follow-Up Document

When saving a returns order, you can create a quality notification in quality management to trigger a parallel quality management process. The necessary settings for output management using output determination (SD-BF-OC) to create the quality notifications are already maintained.

🙌Valuation with Reference to Preceding Sales Process

Traditionally, return deliveries are valuated based on current material valuation.
Now, S/4HANA 2025 allows you to reuse the valuation of the original outbound delivery, which is critical when:

👉Sales context changed over time

👉Historical valuation is required

👉Margin or profitability analysis must stay accurate

This is enabled via a customizing switch and applied at the document level.

📌Note: The checkbox into the Customer Return item level is currently not available in Fiori apps like Manage Customer Returns or Version 2.  It is accessible through the Create Sales Document transaction in SAP GUI, but don’t worry is planned to include into “Manage Customer Returns Version2” in the future.

💡Business Value: It streamlines the return process, resulting in better customer service, while also completing the product's journey internally or forwarding them to the supplier if necessary.

 

☘️ Joule: AI-Driven Sales Insights

SAP continues expanding Joule’s embedded AI capabilities. For Sales, new features include:

👉Faster insights into the order fulfillment cycle

👉Assistance with delivery and supply issue resolution

👉Guided billing document creation

👉Support to streamline the solution quotation process

💡Business Value: These capabilities help both sales and customer service teams act faster and with more context.

For Joule capabilities in detail, you can visit: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | SAP Help Portal

🎯Final Thoughts

SAP S/4HANA 2025 brings meaningful advancements to the Sales line of business by strengthening output flexibility, enhancing extensibility through RAP-based APIs, improving efficiency in order creation, and providing smarter returns process.
Combined with Joule’s growing AI insights, organizations gain more automation, transparency, and decision support than ever before.

 

I hope this was helpful for you 😊

I'm looking forward to your feedback and thoughts. You can post your questions in the SAP community, referring to this blog post.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team.

entry.title: How AI Automation Eliminates Master Data Duplicates in SAP
entry.content:
The Business Case

Whether running SAP ECC or S/4HANA, most organizations struggle with duplicate master data for materials, vendors, and customers. This isn't just a "clutter" problem, poor material master data leads to users creating Purchase Requisitions with text items, bypassing procurement controls and ruining spend analytics.

Let’s explore how AI automation and data vectorization can clean up the core and prevent duplicates before they happen.

The Engine: What is Data Vectorization?

At Skybuffer, we are constantly exploring the benefits of SAP data vectorization. Simply put, vectorization converts data into numerical arrays (vectors) using embedding functions.

Unlike standard SAP keyword searches, vectors are language-independent and allow algorithms to perform similarity searches. While standard tables see rows and columns, a vectorized model sees the relationship between data points.

How Does It Help?

When data is vectorized, we can execute hybrid searches that are indifferent to word order or exact spelling.

Example: Imagine searching for a global customer like "Skybuffer." You might have different accounts for Poland and Norway. If a user tries to create a new record, they can simply ask:

"Do we have a Customer master record for Skybuffer in Poland?"

By querying vectorized KNA1 table data, the AI identifies the record even if the naming convention differs slightly.

 Pic.1 Hybrid Search based on Vectorized KNA1 Table Data

Real-World Integration Points

Hybrid search is most powerful when embedded directly into the SAP business process:

  • Preventative Checks: During the "Save" action in SAP, a custom check against vectorized data can alert the user to a potential duplicate.

  • External Access: Quick searches via Microsoft Teams or SAP Fiori to find existing masters on the go.

  • Smart Procurement: Identifying an existing Material Master during the ordering process, preventing the user from resorting to a "text item."

 Pic.2 Quick Searches of SAP Master Data based on Vectorized Data

Conclusion

Generative AI is a powerhouse when used wisely. Many companies struggle to find the right use cases, often just mirroring existing SAP processes in a chat window. That isn’t true AI automation.

Our approach is different: we focus on the gaps in standard automation. By using AI to simplify data discovery, we reduce development effort and provide an intelligent, frictionless experience for SAP users.

Closing Statement

Keep automating. Remember: smart customization is a competitive advantage, not a deviation from the standard. Build with AI in mind to slash support costs and maximize your ROI via the Skybuffer AI platform.

entry.title: Build a Winning IT Operating Model for Your S/4HANA Investment
entry.content:
Werde zum Value Partner: Entfessele das volle Potenzial der S/4HANA-Investition mit dem richtigen IT Operating Model!

Die digitale Transformation ist immer noch für viele Unternehmen ein zentrales strategisches Ziel – und SAP S/4HANA spielt dabei in vielen Unternehmen eine Schlüsselrolle. Ein maßgeschneidertes IT Operating Model (ITOM) ist die Voraussetzung dafür, dass die Potenziale von S/4HANA voll ausgeschöpft werden können. Dieser Beitrag zeigt, worauf es beim Aufbau eines ITOM im SAP-Kontext ankommt – und welche strategischen Entscheidungen dabei zu treffen sind.​ 

Ausgangslage 

Ein wirksames ITOM entsteht nicht im luftleeren Raum. Vielmehr hängt es entscheidend vom Reifegrad der bestehenden SAP-Landschaft und Organisation ab:​ 

  • SAP bereits umfänglich im Einsatz: Wenn SAP bereits tief in der Organisation verankert ist, existieren in der Regel klare Verantwortlichkeiten, Prozesse und ein grundlegendes Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien.​ 
  • SAP als Nischenlösung: Wird SAP nur in einzelnen Bereichen z.B. in der Buchhaltung eingesetzt, sind die Erfahrungen mit SAP zu arbeiten mit etwas Geschick auch auf andere Bereiche z.B. Produktionsplanung, Logistik übertragbar. Schließlich weiß die Organisation bereits, wie SAP "tickt". 
  • SAP als Neuland: Hier muss sich erst ein breites Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien entwickeln im Projektverlauf und nach dem go-live im Betrieb. In Unternehmen ohne SAP-Vorerfahrung ist zusätzlich in Change-Management, Qualifizierungsmaßnahmen und kulturelle Integration zu investieren.​ 

Je nach Ausgangssituation müssen IT-Verantwortliche ihr ITOM gezielt ausrichten – sowohl organisatorisch als auch kulturell. Der enge Schulterschluss mit den Fachbereichen ist essenziell. In allen drei Ausgangssituationen hat die S/4 HANA-Einführung eine Vielzahl von Implikationen z.B. auf die Governance,  auf die Skalierbarkeit, auf den Einsatz neuer Technologien, Arbeitsweisen und Prozesse.​ 

Kritische Erfolgsdimensionen für das ITOM​ 

Beim Design des Operating Models müssen verschiedene Schlüsselbereiche berücksichtigt werden. Viele Darstellungen beschränken sich auf die 3 Dimensionen Prozesse, Technologie und Mensch. Erfahrungsgemäß lohnt sich eine genauere Aufteilung der Schlüsselbereiche 

  • Architektur: Häufig bereits zu Projektbeginn definiert, bildet sie eine feste Rahmenbedingung – sollte aber regelmäßig auf strategische Passfähigkeit geprüft werden.​ 
  • Tooling & IT-Prozesse: Von DevOps über Monitoring bis hin zu Support-Prozessen – effiziente Toolchain und klar definierte Prozesse z.B. nach ITIL sind unerlässlich.​ 
  • Organisationsstruktur: Ob zentralisiert, dezentral oder hybrid – die Struktur muss zur operativen Realität passen.​ 
  • Prozessansatz: Funktionale oder End-to-End-Ausrichtung oder beides? Der gewählte Ansatz hat direkte Auswirkungen auf Verantwortlichkeiten und Schnittstellen.​ 
  • Way of Working: Agile, klassische oder hybride Arbeitsweisen beeinflussen das Design der Rollen und Aufgabenverteilung.​ 
  • Change Management, Ausbildung, Kompetenz: Mit Unsicherheiten und Skepsis in der Belegschaft sollte offensiv umgegangen werden. Eine fokussierte Change-Begleitung ist sehr zu empfehlen für den Gesamterfolg der Transformation. 

Nicht alle dieser Bereiche sind frei gestaltbar – viele sind durch bestehende IT-Strategien oder Unternehmensvorgaben determiniert. Daher ist es essenziell, zu Beginn des ITOM-Designs zwischen fixen Rahmenbedingungen und flexibel gestaltbaren Elementen zu unterscheiden. Mit einer S/4HANA Transformation besteht die unbedingte Möglichkeit, Strukturen zu hinterfragen und "alte Zöpfe" neu zu ordnen.​ 

Wie sich die verschiedenen Schlüsselbereiche in den Projekt- und Betriebskontext einordnen lassen und welche Aufgaben der/die ITOM-Verantwortliche währende die S/4 HANA Einführung hat, zeigt die folgende Tabelle in einer Übersicht. 

 Schlüsselbereiche für den CCOE Aufbau

Strukturierter Aufbau: Vorgehensmodell zur ITOM-Entwicklung​ 

Folgende Schritte haben sich in der Praxis bewährt:​ 

1. Benennung eines verantwortlichen ITOM Leads:  

  • Sicherstellung klarer Verantwortlichkeiten für Design und Umsetzung des Operating Models. 
  • Verankerung der Rolle auf ausreichender Hierarchieebene (z. B. direkt unterhalb der CIO-/IT-Leitung).​ 
  • Aufbau eines schlagkräftigen Kernteams mit Vertretern aus relevanten Domänen (z. B. Architektur, Betrieb, Prozesse).​ 

2. Definition von Zielbild, Prioritäten und Vision​ 

  • Erarbeitung eines klaren Zielbilds für das zukünftige ITOM: Rolle, Beitrag zur Gesamtstrategie, Abgrenzung.​ 
  • Festlegung von Leitzielen wie z. B. Skalierbarkeit, Automatisierung oder Business-Alignment.​ 
  • Identifikation und Priorisierung der größten Hebel und Handlungsfelder – z.B. auch die Etablierung neuer “Spielregeln” wie Clean Core, SAP BTP first bei Erweiterungen, durchgängige Tool Chain 

(3. Optional) Bestandsaufnahme des aktuellen TOM (As-Is)​ 

  • Erhebung vorhandener Rollen, Prozesse, Tools und Strukturen (ggf. durch Interviews, Workshops, Dokumentenanalyse).​
  • Bewertung der Reifegrade in den einzelnen ITOM-Dimensionen.​ 
  • Identifikation von Redundanzen, Lücken, Pain Points oder auch Best Practices im aktuellen Setup.​ 

4. Definition zentraler Aufgabenbereiche (Building Blocks siehe Grafik)​ 

  • Clusterung aller ITOM-Aufgabenbereiche nach strategischen, Governance- und operativen Fähigkeiten - z.B. Sourcingstrategie, Business- und IT-Zusammenarbeit, Applikations- und SAP-Basis Betrieb 
  • Orientierung an IT-Prozessen und –Funktionen sowie der Business- und IT Zusammenarbeit. 
  • Klare Abgrenzung der Aufgabenbereiche zur Vermeidung von Verantwortlichkeitslücken.​ 

Aufgabenbereiche im S/4 HANA Betrieb

5. Erste Zuordnung von Rollen zu Aufgabenbereichen (High-Level)​ 

  • Benennung von Rollen pro Aufgabenbereich – z. B. Produkt Owner, Architekt, Applikations-/Prozess-Experte.​ 
  • Berücksichtigung vorhandener Rollen in der Organisation zur Vermeidung von Doppelstrukturen.​ 
  • Differenzierung zwischen Rollen und Stellen. 

6. Detaillierung von Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten​ 

  • Detaillierung der Aufgabenbereiche und Erstellung eines RACI-Modells zur klaren Aufgabenverteilung (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed).​ 
  • Beschreibung der Rollenprofile inklusive benötigter Kompetenzen, KPIs und Zielbilder.​ 
  • Validierung der Rollenzuordnung mit Fachbereichen, Betriebs- und Projektorganisationen. 

7. Identifikation von Lernbedarfen und Kompetenzlücken​ 

  • Ableitung von Trainingspfaden für bestehende Mitarbeitende basierend auf neuem Rollen- und Skillbedarf.​ 
  • Aufbau von Lernformaten - z. B. learning journeys, E-Learnings, SAP Learning Hub.​ 
  • Identifikation von Bedarf an Neueinstellungen oder externem Know-how - z. B. in Architektur, DevOps. 

8. Definition der Zielorganisation und Teamstrukturen​ 

  • Entwicklung eines Organisationsdesigns inkl. Teamgrößen, Berichtslinien und Verantwortlichkeiten - z.B. was gehört in das SAP CCOE und was nicht.
  • Entscheidung über Betriebsmodelle - z. B. zentral vs. dezentral, Inhouse vs. Outsourcing. 
  • Verankerung der ITOM-Strukturen im Operating Model der Gesamt-IT.
  • Organisatorischer Umgang mit neuen Themen z.B. Künstliche Intelligenz 

Ein iterativer, faktenbasierter Ansatz stellt sicher, dass das Operating Model nicht nur auf dem Papier funktioniert, sondern im operativen Alltag tragfähig ist.​ 

​ ...

Fazit​ 

Ein zielgerichtetes Operating Model für SAP S/4HANA ist ein zentraler Erfolgsfaktor für die digitale Transformation der IT-Landschaft. Es ermöglicht nicht nur einen stabilen und skalierbaren Betrieb der SAP-Plattform, sondern schafft auch die Grundlage für Innovation und Agilität. IT-Verantwortliche sollten die Gestaltung des ITOM daher als strategische Aufgabe begreifen – mit klarer Vision, strukturierter Umsetzung und konsequenter Verankerung in der Organisation. 

 ...

Torsten Scheffler ist selbständiger Berater und hat über 200 CCOE Projekte durchgeführt. 

...

Useful links: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/building-high-performing-sap-it-organization-the-art-of-organizational/ba-p/13464999

entry.title: Stop Building from Scratch: Autonomous AI Agents for SAP Sales Order Processing
entry.content:
Why this article?

It is great that we are living in the era of AI technologies where humans are still needed to design and automate 😀. But let’s be honest: give it a few more years and no company will ask "Which AI platform should we choose?" That decision will be made by central AI services based on strict country and corporate regulations. The only thing that will matter is security and reliability.

So, why am I writing this? Because I see so many people struggling with custom, built-from-scratch AI automation solutions at the enterprise level. These projects start quickly, but they get stuck just as fast.

You know, it is like cookies 😀. You can make one in a sandbox; however, to scale that to a corporate production level is a totally different problem.

If you are still wondering why this article matters: I want to highlight that while humans are still designing automation, speed is everything. Deploying autonomous AI agents on a solid, reliable platform is a massive competitive advantage. Those playing around with internal IT projects or relying on System Integrators (SI) to build from zero are being left behind.

Let me explain why using one simple example: Customer Purchase Orders.

The Business Case: The Reality of POs

A company wins a deal (congratulations!), and the Customer sends a Purchase Order (PO).

In an ideal world, this happens via EDI. If you have EDI, you are lucky and you can stop reading. But in the real world, customers send PDFs, scanned images with handwriting, Excel files with macros, or even a screenshot of their laptop screen.

This shouldn't be an issue. Innovative platforms must handle data extraction from unstructured sources out of the box.

“Training? Fine-tuning??”

I know some of you just thought that 😊. Yes, training OCR models used to be mandatory. If you haven't faced the pain of training models, be happy, because you are young enough 😊. Today, this must work without that hassle.

The Main Challenge: Master Data Mapping

Extracting text is easy. The real challenge is my favorite one: Master Data Mapping.

How do we robustly and reliably read the customer’s product description and map it to the correct Master Data Object in our SAP system? And how do we do it without heavy development?

The Solution: SAP Master Data Vectorization

We don't need custom code; we need Vectorization.

Generative AI can write SQL queries, sure. But think about it: how many SQL queries would the AI have to guess to find a material that is semantically similar but written differently? Infinite. Or maybe a few less if you are lucky and the description contains an acronym like "A4 Paper" 😊.

When AI looks for a material master in vectorized data (rather than a database table), it reads by sense, not just by keywords. This allows it to reliably find the right SAP object even if the customer's description is vague.

I know only one SAP-certified Business AI Platform that has this Vectorization feature in its standard set: Skybuffer AI.

 Pic.1 SAP Master Data Vectorization Setup

 Pic.2 Example of Material Master Similarities based Search

The Implementation: Autonomous Agents in Action

This is the essential part: stop playing in the sandbox. Take a platform and create a simple autonomous AI agent.

In this example, I am using Skybuffer AI because it handles the heavy lifting out of the box:

  • Vectorizes SAP master data for semantic search.

  • Reads Emails (including complex attachments and OCR).

  • Safety First: Uses Mailboxes as a staging area (no messy data replication).

  • ABAP Control: It doesn't let the AI "guess" how to create data. It passes structured JSON to an ABAP class/BAPI for safe execution.

  • No Code: configured entirely in SAP Fiori.

The Flow

A Skybuffer AI agent acts effectively as an automated employee. You can schedule it, give it tools, and let it run.

 Pic.3 Scheduling Autonomous AI Agent

Step 1: The Input

The Agent connects to a Mailbox. It reads the email, extracts the attachment data, and places it into the Agent's memory.

 Pic.4 Flow inside the Tool of Skybuffer AI Action Server

Step 2: The Brain (Generative AI)

We connect the Generative AI and give it guidelines.

Note: The vectorized SAP master data is mapped directly to this action.

 Pic.5 Generative AI Action of Skybuffer AI Action Server

The AI creates a JSON structure based on our request. Crucially, we do not ask the AI to update SAP directly. We hand that JSON over to an SAP ABAP class. This ensures the transactional data is created correctly, without hallucinations.

Step 3: The Output

We update the email in the Mailbox with the status and send a confirmation back to the customer.

Everything runs either in your SAP HANA On-Premise shell (directly in your network) or on your SAP BTP tenant.

Conclusion

Autonomous AI agents are no longer "future tech", they are easy solutions available now.

By choosing a Business AI automation platform rather than building from scratch, your company gets the best of Generative AI and the SAP backend immediately.

It is strange that so many companies are still "playing" with in-house development. You wouldn't build your own Sales Order Management system from scratch instead of using SAP, right? 😊 That would be weird. Yet, companies are giving huge budgets to internal teams to build AI wrappers.

You don't need a giant team. You need a reliable solution, speed, and flexibility. Just look at Cursor, they revolutionized coding with roughly 60 employees before their massive 900M funding round 😉.

Be quick. Be autonomous.

entry.title: Transport Mechanism of Key-User Extensibility Objects in Public Cloud & Private Cloud
entry.content:
We are now quite familiar with the concept of Key User Extensibility/In-App Extensibility while working in a S/4 HANA Cloud environment. Key User Extensibility is available in both the environments of S/4 HANA Cloud (Public Edition & Private Edition) and is the preferred mode of creating extension due to its highest Clean Core level and being lightweight.

There are many blogposts available in the community showing creation and usage of the most commonly used Key User Extensibility Objects like Custom Fields / Custom Business Objects / Custom CDS Views etc.

However, in this blog post we will discuss about the difference in approach to Transport the Key User Extensibility objects to higher environments (Dev -> Test/QA ->Prod) in S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition & S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition .

Considering a use case of  Custom fields for demonstration. Although for creation and modification of custom fields, the approach is similar in both Private and Public cloud but there are few differences in approach while it comes to transporting them and one of the key differences being -

A transport process for Key User Extensibility objects in Private Cloud are  associated with an actual Transport Request number while in Public Cloud there is no actual Transport Request number associated while transporting them.

In this blogpost we will focus on the Transportation part but however for explanation I have added brief details on the creation process too.

S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition

S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition

Creation -

Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

 

Create the new custom field for the required business context and activate it for the required areas where you want to use this custom field -

 



 

After Enabling Usage for the required areas, click on Publish for the field to be available on the selected contexts.

 

If you want to add this field to your Fiori Application’s UI then you can do that using the option Adapt UI from the particular Fiori application for which you have created and exposed the custom field.

 

 

*Same as Public Cloud in this context
Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

The steps for activating the usage of the custom field and publishing are also same in S/4 HANA Private Cloud as shown in the other column for Public Cloud so you can follow all the same screenshots given in the left column for Private Cloud environment also.

**Screenshots have not been repeated.

Transport process -

No actual Transport Request number associated. Transport of Key User Extensibility objects happens using two Fiori Applications –

 Export Software Collection (To be used in Source System)

 Import Collection (To be used in Target system)

 

Transport happens with actual Transport Request number with two Fiori applications used –

 Configure Software Packages

 Register Extensions for Transport

Transportation process in Source system -

The transportation process is done by Software Collection.

The first step is to create a Software Collection and add the Custom field in the collection. This is done by the Fiori App – Export Software Collection

 

Once you create a Software collection you have to add your Custom field or whichever Extensibility object you are planning to move by clicking on Add Items

 

 

You can filter your Objects by selecting the type of objects you want to add in the Software Collection

 

 

Once everything is added you can click on Check and then finally Export

 

First step go to the Fiori Application Configure Software Packages and add registration for your package by clicking on the button Add Registration

 

 

Add your Transport Number from the F4 search help available under the column Transport Request to assign the package to a particular Transport number available.
Optionally you can turn on the Automatic Request handling for this package with the option available.

 

 

Second step go to the Fiori Application Register Extensions For Transport

 

Select your Custom field or the Extensibility object that you want to transport and click on Reassign to Package and select the package you registered in previous step.

 

 

 

By default your Extensibility object will get assigned to the Transport to which the package is assigned but you can change the transport assignment by clicking on the option Assign to Transport Request.

You can now go to Transaction Code SE10 and check the transport number under your name. The Custom field or the  extensibility object that you added will be available in the Transport Request .

You can release and move the transport to upper environments based on the transport mechanism that is being followed in your project.

Transportation process in Target system -

Based on architecture if it is a 3-Tier Public Cloud system then the next higher environment is going to be the Test Environment in Public Cloud.

In the Test Environment open the application Import Collection

 

 

 

Select your Software collection that you exported from source system and click on Check and Import

 

 

If you want to move your Software Collection to production environment then you can move it by the Forward option in the Import Collection app

 

No such similar activity is required here. Transport will be moved and available in QA/Test environment with transport release mechanism followed.

 

**Additional information –

Use the Fiori Application Extensibility Inventory in both the S/4 HANA Cloud versions to check dependencies of objects before transporting.

entry.title: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition
entry.content:
This blog provides a curated collection of how‑to guides, practical tips, and step‑by‑step instructions based on real requests we have received from users working with the Discovery Package for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition. Its purpose is to help others facing similar questions complete their tasks more efficiently. In addition, several entries highlight known limitations or issues encountered in the process—offering transparency and guidance on how to navigate them. The hub continues to grow as new questions and requests come in.

Topics covered

  • Client Copy 

  • Change ADT to logon to a different client

  • Http error 429

 

Client Copy

It is possible to run a local client copy in the Discovery package system using TA SCCLN. TA SCC_CLIENT_SIZE can be used to check on the size of a client. Due to HANA compression, this is only an approximation if more than one client is filled. The tool also supports selective size determination for specified tables. As an example client 100 size info looks like this:  

Some useful tips: 

  • Client copies must no longer be executed from the target client. Instead, we recommend to run the Client Copy tool from a third client which is not affected by the tool. For example, when copying from client 100 to 200, you could run the tool in client 000. 

  • The client copy always locks the target client. By default, the source client is also locked. You may choose to not lock the source client at the risk of concurrent write access to the source client resulting in inconsistent copies. A client lock now not only prevents new logins using SAP GUI, but also for HTTP access. 

Here is an example of creating a new Client 101 and copying Client 100 into it. Using profile SAP_ALL copies almost all information. This is the pic after the copy.  

 

Change ADT to logon to a different client

If you want to logon ADT to a different client than the default client 100 (e.g. after a client copy) you can configure it as follows: 

In the https logon method, ADT uses the client specified in the profile parameter login/system_client -- client 100 in our system. This can be changed to a different client in TA RZ10 and requires a restart (see sevice requests in SAP for Me). Afterwards, ADT/Fiori/WebGUI will use this one as default logon client.  In Fiori and WebGui you can still specify other clients on the logon screen or by adding ?sap-client=xxx to the URL. 

 

Http Error 429

When an HTTP 429 error occurs, it means the HTTP client has sent too many requests within a short period of time. 

The TDD Discovery Package system is configured with a fixed capacity of 32 CPUs and 256 GB RAM. 
If an HTTP 429 error appears, you need to reduce the number of parallel requests, for example by lowering the number of simultaneous logons. 

Please note that the TDD system is not designed for load or performance testing.

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur - User Profile Management
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Recommendations
  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub
  • AI Assisted E-Document Error Handling : Continuous Adaptation

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.
